examples.x: She always drags her feet when it comes to completing her assignments, causing delays in the team.
He dragged his feet in making a decision, which resulted in missed opportunities.
They will likely drag their feet on implementing the new policy, causing further frustration among employees.
I'm tired of waiting for him; he keeps dragging his feet on finalizing the contract.
She always drags her heels when it comes to attending meetings, causing frustration among her colleagues.
He dragged his heels in submitting the report, resulting in a missed deadline.
They will likely drag their heels on implementing the new software, causing delays in the project timeline.

senses.definition: in a suspended state to be considered and dealt with in the future

examples.x: The project is currently on hold due to budget constraints.
She put her travel plans on hold until she could save enough money.
They will likely put the decision on hold until they gather more information.
The team is putting the meeting on hold while they wait for an important participant to join.
The company has put the expansion plans on hold due to market uncertainties.

senses.definition: refusal or cancellation of an invitation, offer, or plan with intention of accepting it or doing it at another time instead

examples.x: I'm sorry, but I'll have to give you a rain check on joining you for the movie tonight.
Due to a scheduling conflict, I'll need to give you a rain check on attending the conference.
Can we do a rain check on our coffee date?
I'm afraid I'll have to ask for a rain check on the dinner reservation.
I apologize, but I'll need to give you a rain check on helping out with the event tomorrow.

senses.definition: to put something off in order to gain more time

examples.x: She buys time by requesting an extension for the project deadline.
They bought time by postponing the decision until they had more information.
We will buy time by implementing temporary measures while we develop a long-term solution.
The team is buying time by engaging in negotiations to extend the contract.
He was buying time by stalling the meeting until he could gather his thoughts.
The company has bought time by securing interim funding to sustain operations.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈɪk ðə kæn dˌaʊn ðə ɹˈoʊd

senses.definition: to keep postponing problems one must deal with

examples.x: They often kick the can down the road instead of addressing the underlying issues.
The government kicked the can down the road by postponing the decision on the controversial policy.
If they keep kicking the can down the road, they will eventually have to face the consequences.
She is currently kicking the can down the road by avoiding the difficult conversation.
They were kicking the can down the road while hoping the problem would resolve itself.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is temporarily inactive or postponed

examples.x: They decide to put the project on ice until they secure additional funding.
She put her travel plans on ice due to unforeseen circumstances.
We will put the event on ice until we can find a better venue.
The project is currently on ice until further notice.
They kept the idea on ice for a while before deciding to move forward.
The event will be on ice until a suitable venue is secured.
The negotiations are currently on ice pending further discussions.

senses.definition: to refrain from taking action when one should

examples.x: She sits on her hands and avoids addressing the pressing issue at hand.
They sat on their hands while the opportunity for change slipped away.
The team will be sitting on their hands if they don't take proactive measures to address the problem.
He is currently sitting on his hands instead of taking action and resolving the conflict.
They were sitting on their hands while the situation deteriorated further.
By this time next year, they will be sitting on their hands if they don't initiate necessary reforms.

senses.definition: used to show indecision over something

examples.x: She is currently on the fence about which university to attend.
They were on the fence about whether to invest in the stock market or not.
The team will be on the fence until they gather more information about the proposed project.
He is currently on the fence regarding his career path and is exploring different options.
By this time next year, they will still be on the fence about whether to start their own business.
The committee has been on the fence about approving the new policy for months.
By the end of the month, they will have been on the fence for a year, unable to make a final decision.

word_list.word: [put] off (until|till) tomorrow what {sb} can do today
word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ˈɔf ʌntˈɪl tˈɪl təmˈɔːɹoʊ wˌʌt ˌɛsbˈiː kæn dˈuː tədˈeɪ

senses.definition: to decide to do something that needs to taken care of right now at another time

examples.x: She is putting off until tomorrow what she can do today, prolonging the completion of her tasks.

senses.definition: to try to delay the occurrence of something so that one can prevent it from happening or have more time to properly prepare for it

examples.x: He plays for time during negotiations to gather more information and assess his options.
They played for time by delaying their response, hoping for a more favorable outcome.
The team will play for time in the upcoming meeting to buy themselves more preparation time.
She is playing for time by asking for an extension, hoping for a breakthrough in the negotiations.
They were playing for time by engaging in lengthy discussions, trying to delay the decision.
By this time next month, they will still be playing for time, waiting for the market conditions to improve.
